jokes.text: When Vivian asks if she's just a bustline to him, Philip responds 'Did you say something, honey?'
jokes.setup: Vivian is upset about being objectified and asks a serious question
jokes.punchline: Philip proves her point by not listening because he's still distracted
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that deepens the initial joke. Philip's obliviousness escalates the comedy while staying true to character.

jokes.text: Will suggests Carlton's musical taste came from Vivian listening to 'It's a Small World After All' while pregnant
jokes.setup: Will explains babies can hear music in the womb
jokes.punchline: Implies Carlton's annoying personality comes from annoying Disney music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration - explains Carlton's personality through a creative biological 'origin story' that fits perfectly.

jokes.text: Vivian tells Will 'Hands off the fetus'
jokes.setup: Will is trying to influence the unborn baby with music and names
jokes.punchline: Vivian delivers stern parental boundary with clinical terminology
jokes.explanation: Memorable line that became quotable. The medical term 'fetus' in a casual family argument is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: Will suggests the African name 'Brother Outaphilia'
jokes.setup: Will wants to give the baby a righteous African name
jokes.punchline: 'Brother Outaphilia' sounds African but means 'out of Philly'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that references Will's Philadelphia origins while sounding authentically African. Shows character growth and cultural awareness.

jokes.text: Brother Outaphilia
jokes.setup: Will wants to give the baby an African name
jokes.punchline: The name sounds African but is actually 'Out of Philly' referencing his own background
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly integrates Will's Philadelphia background with his attempt at cultural consciousness. High craft score for the clever construction.

jokes.text: Will says about Hilary and Carlton: 'they a couple of wings short of a bucket'
jokes.setup: Will explains his interest in influencing the new baby
jokes.punchline: Describes the other kids as incomplete/defective using KFC metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both character-specific (Will's street vernacular) and universally understood. Efficient insult delivery.

jokes.text: Hilary immediately proves Will's point by asking Philip to increase her credit card limit
jokes.setup: Philip defends Hilary and Carlton as perfectly fine
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately demonstrates her dependency and poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and irony. The joke structure of setup-immediate contradiction is flawlessly executed.

jokes.text: V-neck!
jokes.setup: Carlton says he's decided to 'throw caution to the wind'
jokes.punchline: His idea of wild rebellion is wearing a V-neck sweater
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Carlton's conservative nature. The economy is perfect - one word delivers the entire joke. High character integration makes this memorable.

jokes.text: Will responds 'Coincidence? I think not.'
jokes.setup: Two examples of Hilary and Carlton's issues just occurred
jokes.punchline: Will delivers a mock-serious conspiracy theorist catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that punctuates the sequence. Pop culture reference is well-timed but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Philip asks if he has to sit through Carlton's Urkel impression again
jokes.setup: Carlton wants to practice something on Philip
jokes.punchline: Philip assumes it's another Steve Urkel impression
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows Carlton's annoying habits. Pop culture reference dates the show but works in context.

jokes.text: Philip tells Carlton 'No, and get some Binaca' after Carlton stares him in the eye
jokes.setup: Carlton tries negotiation technique of intense eye contact
jokes.punchline: Philip rejects the request and critiques Carlton's breath
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with unexpected punchline. Philip's bluntness about Carlton's breath is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Carlton asks for $75,000 allowance raise, justifying it as 'The book says start high'
jokes.setup: Carlton is practicing negotiation techniques from a book
jokes.punchline: He takes 'start high' to an absurd extreme with $75,000
jokes.explanation: Carlton's literal interpretation of advice creates absurd situation. The amount is so ridiculous it works.

jokes.text: I'd like a raise in my allowance. - All right, how much? - $75,000.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking for allowance raise using negotiation book
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous amount that's more than many salaries
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton disconnect from reality - treating allowance like corporate salary

jokes.text: Carlton says 'Now I can stop taking money from your wallet' when Philip offers $3 more
jokes.setup: Carlton seems grateful for the small raise
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been stealing from Philip's wallet
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Carlton's casual confession of theft is unexpected and well-structured.

jokes.text: Hilary says 'This is the last time I shop here for fruit' about the grocery store
jokes.setup: Hilary is learning to grocery shop independently
jokes.punchline: She treats grocery shopping like shopping at different boutiques
jokes.explanation: Pure Hilary - misunderstands how grocery stores work. Shows her privileged disconnect from normal activities.

jokes.text: Carlton suggests putting a bell around Hilary's neck so they know when she's coming
jokes.setup: Hilary has been causing disruptions
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests treating her like livestock with a bell
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult with good visual imagery. Carlton's rare moment of wit at Hilary's expense.

jokes.text: When asked what she has to say for herself, Hilary responds 'I'm not pregnant'
jokes.setup: Philip lists Hilary's failures and asks for her defense
jokes.punchline: She offers the non-accomplishment of not being pregnant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Perfect Hilary logic - she cites not being pregnant as an achievement when it's just a basic status.

jokes.text: Hilary asks 'Why don't you just beat me with a wire hanger?' when told to get a job
jokes.setup: Philip tells Hilary she needs to get a job
jokes.punchline: She compares job-hunting to child abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reference to 'Mommie Dearest.' Shows Hilary's dramatic overreaction and privilege.

jokes.text: Will brags about being on the news 'without handcuffs'
jokes.setup: Will announces he'll be on the 6 o'clock news
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's not for criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment acknowledging stereotypes about young Black men. Self-aware without being preachy.

jokes.text: Will says the protest 'would even made Gandhi proud. Except we went out for burgers afterwards'
jokes.setup: Will compares their protest to Gandhi's peaceful resistance
jokes.punchline: Undercuts the comparison by mentioning they went for burgers
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference with good self-deprecating punchline. Shows Will's self-awareness about teenage activism.

jokes.text: Will admits his social consciousness comes from a girl on the swim team teaching him the breaststroke
jokes.setup: Will explains his noble community involvement
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually motivated by a romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection revealing teenage boy's real motivations. 'Breaststroke' adds slight double entendre.

jokes.text: Yeah, polyester.
jokes.setup: News reporter says protesters all have one thing in common
jokes.punchline: Hilary focuses on their clothing material instead of their cause
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her superficial priorities. High character integration and economy - one word reveals everything about her perspective.

jokes.text: Hilary says the reporter is cute and wonders why she's never seen him, Geoffrey responds 'Probably 'cause this is the news and he's never been on Studs'
jokes.setup: Hilary wonders why she hasn't seen the attractive reporter before
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey points out she only watches trashy TV shows
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation about Hilary's viewing habits. 'Studs' reference dates the show but was topical then.

jokes.text: After Hilary's self-pity about having no job and being nobody, the family shouts 'Shut up!' because they're trying to watch TV
jokes.setup: Hilary delivers melodramatic monologue about her worthlessness
jokes.punchline: Family's annoyed reaction shows they care more about TV than her feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing interrupts Hilary's self-pity with family's selfish priorities. The collective response amplifies the comedy.

jokes.text: Will raps on TV news: 'I'm for the rec center because that's where I do most of my rec centering'
jokes.setup: Will is being interviewed about supporting the rec center
jokes.punchline: Creates the nonsensical verb 'rec centering'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds like something a teenager would say on TV. Shows Will's quick wit under pressure.

jokes.text: When I got home late from the grocery store, Mama said, 'Lock him out!' Now I know why Pops had to go 'Cause Mama is way whacked out!
jokes.setup: Will transitions from interview to impromptu rap
jokes.punchline: His rap reveals family dysfunction while trying to promote community unity
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's inability to stay on message, derailing into personal family issues. Good character work showing his impulsive nature.

jokes.text: Will complains about Black people always 'singing or dancing' on TV with 'curlers in their head or out of activator'
jokes.setup: Will is frustrated by his own TV appearance
jokes.punchline: He critiques media representation while having just done exactly what he's complaining about
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on media representation. Will's self-awareness about perpetuating stereotypes he hates is sophisticated writing.

jokes.text: What's activator?
jokes.setup: Will complains about stereotypical portrayals mentioning activator
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent ignorance of Black hair product culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his disconnection from Black cultural references despite being Black himself. High character integration for this cultural divide.

jokes.text: Carlton's nonsensical comparison: 'Was it fair when Bambi's mother died? Or is it fair that the coyote still can't catch the roadrunner?'
jokes.setup: Will complains about life being unfair
jokes.punchline: Carlton compares serious social issues to cartoon logic
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton absurdist logic. Equating cartoon physics with real social issues is perfectly in character and hilariously tone-deaf.

jokes.text: Will responds to Carlton's cartoon philosophy: 'If life was fair, then your legs wouldn't be shorter than your arms'
jokes.setup: Carlton makes philosophical points about cartoon fairness
jokes.punchline: Will turns it personal by mocking Carlton's body proportions
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Carlton's short stature running gag. Will redirects Carlton's abstract philosophy to concrete physical mockery.

jokes.text: Receptionist Carla says about a caller: 'Just another lonely white woman wanting to sleep with you' then 'She's my mother'
jokes.setup: Carla describes a caller in unprofessional terms
jokes.punchline: Reveals the caller is her own mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with dark family dysfunction humor. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When Will tries to compliment Hilary, he struggles and settles on 'your hat always matches your shoes'
jokes.setup: Will tries to list Hilary's good qualities beyond having a job
jokes.punchline: After struggling, he can only come up with her fashion coordination
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Will's struggle to find Hilary's non-material qualities. The pause makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When Hilary 'accidentally' drops her wallet near Trevor, Carla says 'if your horny friend wants to meet Trevor, he's down the hall'
jokes.setup: Hilary obviously drops wallet to get Trevor's attention
jokes.punchline: Carla sees through the act and assumes it's for Will
jokes.explanation: Carla's jaded receptionist persona shines through. Good observational humor about obvious flirting tactics.

jokes.text: But you'll have to fight my mother for him.
jokes.setup: Will asks about meeting Trevor
jokes.punchline: References back to her mother's interest in Trevor
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that maintains the running gag about Carla's mother. Good economy and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Howard says 'Ethnic is definitely a way to go' about Will, misunderstanding his point entirely
jokes.setup: Will explains this isn't about his race
jokes.punchline: Howard immediately makes it about race anyway
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of TV executives' shallow thinking about diversity. Howard's tone-deafness is perfectly characterized.

jokes.text: Mail cart Mike says 'I'm 60 and I'm pushing a mail cart. Do I look like I can help you?'
jokes.setup: Will asks Mike for help getting on air
jokes.punchline: Mike points out his own lack of power/success
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor. Mike's brutal honesty about his position is both funny and poignant.

jokes.text: When asked about weather, Hilary responds 'What happens when it rains? You send Geoffrey out to get the car'
jokes.setup: Howard tests Hilary's weather knowledge
jokes.punchline: She describes weather from a servant-owning perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her privileged worldview. The answer is logical from her perspective but hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: Will protests his treatment and Howard responds 'That's why I'm making her our new weather girl' pointing to Hilary
jokes.setup: Will complains about racial discrimination
jokes.punchline: Howard reveals he's hiring the Black woman (Hilary) instead
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist. Will's discrimination complaint is undercut by Howard hiring another Black person (though for wrong reasons).

jokes.text: Howard says 'I love a screamer' after Hilary gets excited about the job
jokes.setup: Hilary screams with excitement about getting the weather job
jokes.punchline: Howard's response has inappropriate sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Establishes Howard as inappropriately sexual character. The innuendo is obvious but fits his character.

jokes.text: It's 'cause I'm black. That's what the thing is.
jokes.setup: Hilary won't help Will get on air despite owing him
jokes.punchline: Will's exasperated repetition of his discrimination claim, now about his own cousin
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's frustration theme. The repetition works to show his mounting paranoia about discrimination.

jokes.text: Carlton asks 'Haven't you learned anything living with me the past two years?' Will responds 'What, you mean other than the words to Mandy?'
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what Will has learned from him
jokes.punchline: Will's only lesson is memorizing a Barry Manilow song
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Carlton's musical taste. Shows Will has absorbed Carlton's influence in unwanted ways.

jokes.text: Will says about Carlton's previous sharing: 'Last time you shared something with me... I was on antibiotics for two weeks'
jokes.setup: Carlton offers to share his wisdom
jokes.punchline: Will implies Carlton gave him an STD or infection
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about disease transmission. Slightly out of character for the show's usual tone but gets a reaction.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil starts singing 'We Shall Overcome' after being manipulated by Will
jokes.setup: Will has appealed to Philip's civil rights background
jokes.punchline: Philip gets so inspired he starts singing the civil rights anthem
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Philip's inspired state. Shows his genuine connection to civil rights history.

jokes.text: When Hilary describes her new job, Carlton panics: 'Oh, my God! She's gonna be a hooker!'
jokes.setup: Hilary describes a job with good money, short hours, and a limo
jokes.punchline: Carlton jumps to prostitution as the logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Carlton's innocent panic and logical deduction from the clues is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Philip says 'With any luck, she'll be off my property before I'm dead'
jokes.setup: Philip is celebrating Hilary getting a job
jokes.punchline: He hopes to outlive her time living at home
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest parental humor. Philip's blunt assessment of the situation is both harsh and relatable.

jokes.text: Carlton demands Will 'Hop on one foot and say, Carlton is king' for negotiating help
jokes.setup: Will asks Carlton to teach him negotiation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's lesson is demanding public humiliation
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton power trip. Shows his petty nature when he has any leverage over Will.

jokes.text: Will responds 'How about I squeeze your neck till your head pops off?' and Carlton says 'See, you're negotiating already'
jokes.setup: Will threatens violence instead of following Carlton's demand
jokes.punchline: Carlton frames the threat as successful negotiation technique
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where Carlton finds wisdom in Will's threat. Shows Carlton's ability to spin any situation positively.

jokes.text: Carlton advises 'You gotta lie to get what you want. Didn't you watch the Republican convention?'
jokes.setup: Carlton is teaching Will negotiation tactics
jokes.punchline: He references political dishonesty as a teaching example
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor that dates the episode. Carlton's casual acceptance of lying is mildly subversive for his character.

jokes.text: When Will threatens to storm the set, Carla asks 'Will you be needing wardrobe?'
jokes.setup: Will makes dramatic threats about disrupting the newscast
jokes.punchline: Carla treats it as a normal production request
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response that deflates Will's dramatic moment. Carla's professionalism in chaos is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Trevor says about the previous weather person: 'I'm gonna miss Lou.' Someone corrects 'I thought his name was Jack.' Trevor: 'Whatever.'
jokes.setup: Trevor expresses sentiment about the departed weatherman
jokes.punchline: He doesn't even know the person's name and doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment for Trevor's superficiality. His casual indifference to colleagues is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Trevor Collins and these are my real teeth.
jokes.setup: Trevor introduces himself to Hilary
jokes.punchline: His vanity leads him to mention his natural teeth as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures TV anchor vanity with an absurd detail. The specificity of 'real teeth' as a bragging point is both funny and believable for a news anchor.

jokes.text: Trevor's pickup line: 'I can see myself in them. Don't blink.'
jokes.setup: Trevor compliments Hilary's eyes
jokes.punchline: He delivers an awkwardly intense pickup line
jokes.explanation: Standard cheesy pickup line that establishes Trevor's smooth-but-sleazy character. The intensity makes it creepy-funny.

jokes.text: News transition: 'Well, on a brighter note, Jack, our weather man, died.'
jokes.setup: Trevor transitions from a sad homeless story
jokes.punchline: He calls someone's death 'a brighter note'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect news anchor delivery. The casual cruelty is both shocking and hilarious.

jokes.text: Hilary's weather report: 'That's right, girls, it's a frizzy-hair day.'
jokes.setup: Hilary is giving her first professional weather report
jokes.punchline: She relates humidity to hair concerns rather than actual weather impact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary perspective on weather - focuses on vanity concerns rather than practical implications. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Hilary's beach advice: 'remember... you can help keep America beautiful with two simple words... Jenny Craig.'
jokes.setup: Hilary seems to give environmental advice about beach cleanliness
jokes.punchline: She's actually talking about weight loss instead of litter
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Hilary's vanity-focused worldview. Subverts environmental message for shallow beauty concerns.

jokes.text: Howard reacts to Will's hijacking: 'Oh, my God, can we get a close-up? I am so choked up at this moment.'
jokes.setup: Will has disrupted the broadcast with serious social commentary
jokes.punchline: Howard is excited about the dramatic TV moment rather than the message
jokes.explanation: Good satire of TV producer priorities - exploiting genuine emotion for ratings rather than caring about the cause.

jokes.text: Trevor's Freudian slip: 'We will certainly be looking forward to seeing more of your warm front. I mean, the warm front.'
jokes.setup: Trevor is concluding Hilary's weather segment professionally
jokes.punchline: He accidentally sexualizes her weather terminology then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Standard TV anchor sexual innuendo joke. The correction makes it funnier but it's not particularly original.

jokes.text: Hilary, your hair's on fire. - Thanks.
jokes.setup: Will tells Hilary her hair is literally on fire
jokes.punchline: Hilary responds as if it's a compliment, completely missing the danger
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd moment with perfect economy. Hilary's obliviousness to literal danger while treating it as a beauty compliment is perfectly in character and hilariously surreal.

jokes.text: Hilary says goodnight to Trevor who responds 'Good night and Godspeed' - his sign-off
jokes.setup: Hilary says goodnight in a personal way
jokes.punchline: Trevor responds with his professional TV sign-off instead of being personal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Trevor can't turn off his TV persona even in personal moments. Shows his superficiality.
